text.title: Eloquent ORM Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Eloquent ORM methodology for Laravel. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every Eloquent model MUST declare `protected $fillable = [...]`. Empty `$guarded = []` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Empty `$guarded` lets a client inject any field via `Model::create($request->all())`; this is the most common Laravel CVE class.

rule.statement: Accessors and mutators MUST use the `Attribute::make(get: ..., set: ...)` API (Laravel 9+). Legacy `getXAttribute()` / `setXAttribute()` on the same model is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixing the two APIs produces undefined precedence; `Attribute::make` is the single canonical surface.

rule.statement: Eager-load relations at the repository / service boundary via `->with(...)`. Controllers and Resources MUST NOT trigger lazy loading.
rule.rationale: N+1 hidden inside Resource serialisation passes code review; centralising eager-load at the boundary makes cost reviewable.

rule.statement: Multi-write paths (`create` + `relation->attach`, `create` + `update` siblings) MUST be wrapped in `DB::transaction(fn() => ...)`. Side effects MUST fire after commit via `DB::afterCommit`.
rule.rationale: Eloquent does not auto-run transactions for related writes; a failed attach after a successful create leaves orphaned rows.
Silent N+1 regressions ship to prod; strict mode in dev forces the offending line to throw immediately.

rule.statement: Every entity diff MUST land in the same PR with: a Flyway/Laravel migration, a factory update, and an API Resource update where applicable.
rule.rationale: Diffs that touch only the model leave migrations / factories / serializers behind, surfacing as CI failures hours later.
